Workout Complete — R1

The confirmation-and-review screen reached after Finish Workout on the logging screen — closing the loop on Upper Body A. Frame 1 is the summary: sets-logged as the headline number, six per-exercise result rows (moss where logged at or above prescription, neutral otherwise), one quiet Personal Record, and a single Done (#69). Frame 2 shows the same summary with the started/completed times row live in its tappable edit-affordance state — the #65 entry point, whose full editor lives on the Session Detail screen. Judge: does Done read as a dismissal rather than a save, does the PR stay quiet rather than celebratory, and is the timestamp affordance legible without spending a third hue.

➜Run
Timestamp edit affordance (#65 entry)

Finish Workout on the logging screen commits the session; this screen is confirmation and review only — its Done just dismisses, never re-saves (#69). Sets is the headline number: compliance against plan is the one figure worth a hero numeral here, per the app's own no-vanity-metrics rule — duration and total volume ride alongside as supporting values, never competing for the biggest numeral. Each exercise row turns moss the moment it meets or beats its prescription; Dips stayed neutral at 2 of 3 sets — the instrument records what happened, it doesn't round up. The Personal Record row uses the same quiet moss as everywhere else earned work is told, with no accept/dismiss controls — there's nothing left to action. Frame 2 adds the started/completed times in their tappable, accent-outlined edit state (≥44pt) — the only other sanctioned use of accent on this screen, and the seam where #65's full timestamp editor opens from the real Session Detail screen.
